html:
The Life Giving Amulet is a craftable item found in the Magic tab. It requires 3 Gold Nuggets, 2 Nightmare Fuel, and 1 Red Gem to craft and a Prestihatitator to prototype. It can also be found when a Grave is dug up, it can randomly drop from a Slot Machine or be dropped by a Ancient Pseudoscience Station when it is damaged with a Hammer, Gunpowder etc.

If the player dies while having a Life Giving Amulet equipped in the torso slot, they will be resurrected at the beginning of the next day, with an immediate time skip through the night. After resurrection, nearby mobs are put to sleep and the amulet is destroyed.

Resurrection Effects:

  • is set to 50
  • is set to 2/3 of maximum
  • is set to 1/2 of maximum
  • Empty inventory (items can be found at place of death)

When hurt, the Life Giving Amulet will recover 5 at the cost of 5 every 30 seconds when it is worn until fully healed. This reduces the durability of the item by 5% every time the trade-off happens, so it is possible for the amulet to break without reviving the player. Equipped amulets also restore +2 per minute with no effect on durability.

Downloadable Content

In the Shipwrecked DLC, Life Giving Amulets also function as a Life Jacket and save the player from drowning in the Ocean when their boat sinks.

In the Hamlet DLC, Life Giving Amulets can be bought for 50 Oincs at The Flying Pig Arcane Shop in the Pig City.

Tips

  • All bosses and some enemies, such as the Ancient Guardian and Clockwork Bishops, are immune to the sleep effect, so the player may get attacked and die again. It is better to flee from these fights when the player is not in a favorable position.
  • Should the player die, resurrection will occur regardless of how much durability is left, as long as it's above zero. So there is no reason to not use the Amulet to heal wounds until it reaches 5% durability.
  • This item is especially useful when playing as Maxwell. Because of his low maximum Health, Meat Effigies are not recommended, leaving only the Amulet as a way of resurrecting the player when Touch Stones are not available. The amulet also is useful for healing after using the Codex Umbra.

Trivia

  • The Life Giving Amulet was added in The Birds and the Bees & Dogs That Want to Kill You update.
  • The Life Giving Amulet used to be called just "Amulet" before other amulets, such as the Nightmare Amulet, were added. It lost its old texture when it became the Life Giving Amulet, which was similar to the one used for the now defunct Gemology crafting tab.
    • Before the Strange New Powers update, these amulets could be stacked.  As a result of this change, any stacks of amulets previously stored in chests were turned into just one amulet.
    • Before the Strange New Powers update, they required twice the amount of materials to craft.
  • In Don't Starve Together, Klaus uses the older version of the Life Giving Amulet to revive himself.
  • The Life Giving Amulet shares the same in-game model with the Construction Amulet, but its gem is red instead of green.
  • Ironically, though this amulet resurrects the dead, it can still be found in graves.
  • This item could be a reference to the Amulet of Life Saving from NetHack.

Assets

  • Dropped
  • Equipped

Gallery

  • Wilson resurrecting while a nearby Redbird is put to sleep.
  • The old version of the Life Giving Amulet
  • Life Giving Amulet as seen in the poster for The Birds and the Bees & Dogs That Want to Kill You update.
  • Wilson wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Willow wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Wolfgang wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Wendy wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • WX-78 wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Wickerbottom wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Woodie wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Wes wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Maxwell (character) wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Wigfrid wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Webber wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Walani wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Warly wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Wilbur wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Woodlegs wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Wilba wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Wormwood wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Wheeler wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Winona wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Wortox wearing a Life Giving Amulet.
  • Wurt wearing a Life Giving Amulet.

"amulet"
“I feel so safe when I'm wearing it.”
–Wilson
“I'm invincible! Well, maybe not. But it feels good.”
–Willow
“Is very pretty.”
–Wolfgang
“I wonder who used to own this.”
–Wendy
“ANOMALY DETECTED”
–WX-78
“A relic from another time.”
–Wickerbottom
“Always have a backup plan.”
–Woodie
“It channels the darkest energies of the universe.”
–Maxwell
“Finally a way to experience what death is like!”
–Wagstaff
“It's red, and a fighter. Just like me!”
–Wigfrid
“It's a fine necklace, I suppose.”
–Webber
“Looks great, feels great.”
–Walani
“I wear safety.”
–Warly
“A wee bit o'insurance round me neck.”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS SPARKLY THING WHAT GIVETH LIFE!”
–Wilba
“Yay! More life!”
–Wormwood
“Now I can have two lifetimes of adventure.”
–Wheeler
“Jewelry ain't really my thing.”
–Winona
“A glimmering jewel that's rife with life.”
–Wortox
“Pretty magic rock!”
–Wurt
“I'm invincible!!”
–Walter
“I'll breathe easier knowing I have a backup plan.”
–Wanda
The Life Giving Amulet is a craftable item found in the Magic tab. It requires 3 Gold Nuggets, 2 Nightmare Fuel, and 1 Red Gem to craft and a Prestihatitator to prototype. It can also be found when a Grave is dug up, it can randomly drop from a Slot Machine or be dropped by a Ancient Pseudoscience Station when it is damaged with a Hammer, Gunpowder etc.
If the player dies while having a Life Giving Amulet equipped in the torso slot, they will be resurrected at the beginning of the next day, with an immediate time skip through the night. After resurrection, nearby mobs are put to sleep and the amulet is destroyed.
Resurrection Effects:
is set to 50
is set to 2/3 of maximum
is set to 1/2 of maximum
Empty inventory (items can be found at place of death)
When hurt, the Life Giving Amulet will recover 5 at the cost of 5 every 30 seconds when it is worn until fully healed. This reduces the durability of the item by 5% every time the trade-off happens, so it is possible for the amulet to break without reviving the player. Equipped amulets also restore +2 per minute with no effect on durability.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Shipwrecked DLC, Life Giving Amulets also function as a Life Jacket and save the player from drowning in the Ocean when their boat sinks.
In the Hamlet DLC, Life Giving Amulets can be bought for 50 Oincs at The Flying Pig Arcane Shop in the Pig City.
Tips[edit | edit source]
All bosses and some enemies, such as the Ancient Guardian and Clockwork Bishops, are immune to the sleep effect, so the player may get attacked and die again. It is better to flee from these fights when the player is not in a favorable position.
Should the player die, resurrection will occur regardless of how much durability is left, as long as it's above zero. So there is no reason to not use the Amulet to heal wounds until it reaches 5% durability.
This item is especially useful when playing as Maxwell. Because of his low maximum Health, Meat Effigies are not recommended, leaving only the Amulet as a way of resurrecting the player when Touch Stones are not available. The amulet also is useful for healing after using the Codex Umbra.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Life Giving Amulet was added in The Birds and the Bees & Dogs That Want to Kill You update.
The Life Giving Amulet used to be called just "Amulet" before other amulets, such as the Nightmare Amulet, were added. It lost its old texture when it became the Life Giving Amulet, which was similar to the one used for the now defunct Gemology crafting tab.
Before the Strange New Powers update, these amulets could be stacked.  As a result of this change, any stacks of amulets previously stored in chests were turned into just one amulet.
Before the Strange New Powers update, they required twice the amount of materials to craft.
In Don't Starve Together, Klaus uses the older version of the Life Giving Amulet to revive himself.
The Life Giving Amulet shares the same in-game model with the Construction Amulet, but its gem is red instead of green.
Ironically, though this amulet resurrects the dead, it can still be found in graves.
This item could be a reference to the Amulet of Life Saving from NetHack.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Bùa Phục Sinh là một vật phẩm có thể chế tạo được tìm thấy trong tab Phép thuật. Nó cần 3 Vàng Thỏi, 2 Nhiên Liệu Ác Mộng và 1 Ngọc Đỏ để chế tạo và một Máy Mũ Cao Cấp để tạo nguyên mẫu. Nó cũng có thể được tìm thấy khi Mộ được đào lên, nó có thể rơi ngẫu nhiên từ Máy đánh bạc hoặc bị rơi bởi Trạm Giả khoa học Cổ đại khi nó bị hư hại bởi Búa, Thuốc súng, v.v.

It requires 3 [[Gold Nugget]]s, 2 [[Nightmare Fuel]], and 1 [[Red Gem]] to craft and a [[Prestihatitator]] to prototype. It can also be found when a [[Grave]] is [[Shovel|dug]] up, it can randomly drop from a [[Slot Machine]] or be dropped by a [[Ancient Pseudoscience Station]] when it is damaged with a [[Hammer]], [[Gunpowder]] etc. If the player [[death|dies]] while having a Life Giving Amulet equipped in the torso slot, they will be [[Death#Resurrection|resurrected]] at the beginning of the next day, with an immediate time skip through the [[night]]. After resurrection, nearby mobs are put to [[sleep]] and the amulet is destroyed. Resurrection Effects: *[[File:HealthMeter.png|24px|link=Health]] is set to 50 *[[File:HungerMeter.png|24px|link=Hunger]] is set to 2/3 of maximum *[[File:SanityMeter.png|24px|link=Sanity]] is set to 1/2 of maximum *Empty inventory (items can be found at place of death) When hurt, the Life Giving Amulet will recover 5 {{hp}} at the cost of 5 {{hunger}} every 30 seconds when it is worn until fully healed. This reduces the durability of the item by 5% every time the trade-off happens, so it is possible for the amulet to break without reviving the player. Equipped amulets also restore +2 {{sanity}} per minute with no effect on durability. __NOTOC__ == [[File:Gift Icon.png|32px]] Downloadable Content == In the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, Life Giving Amulets also function as a [[Life Jacket]] and save the player from drowning in the [[Ocean]] when their [[boat]] sinks. In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, Life Giving Amulets can be bought for 50 [[Oinc]]s at [[The Flying Pig Arcane Shop]] in the [[Pig City]]. =={{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips== * All [[:Category:Boss Monsters|bosses]] and some enemies, such as the [[Ancient Guardian]] and [[Clockwork Bishop]]s, are immune to the sleep effect, so the player may get attacked and die again. It is better to flee from these fights when the player is not in a favorable position. * Should the player die, resurrection will occur regardless of how much [[durability]] is left, as long as it's above zero. So there is no reason to not use the Amulet to heal wounds until it reaches 5% durability. * This item is especially useful when playing as [[Maxwell]]. Because of his low maximum Health, [[Meat Effigy|Meat Effigies]] are not recommended, leaving only the Amulet as a way of resurrecting the player when [[Touch Stone]]s are not available. The amulet also is useful for healing after using the [[Codex Umbra]]. =={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia== * The Life Giving Amulet was added in ''[[Don't Starve/Version History#November 27, 2012 – The Birds and the Bees & Dogs That Want to Kill You|The Birds and the Bees & Dogs That Want to Kill You]]'' update. * The Life Giving Amulet used to be called just "Amulet" before other amulets, such as the [[Nightmare Amulet]], were added. It lost its old texture when it became the Life Giving Amulet, which was similar to the one used for the now defunct [[Gemology]] crafting tab. ** Before the ''[[Version History#July 2.2C 2013 - Strange New Powers|Strange New Powers]]'' update, these amulets could be stacked.  As a result of this change, any stacks of amulets previously stored in chests were turned into just one amulet. ** Before the ''Strange New Powers'' update, they required twice the amount of materials to craft. * In ''Don't Starve Together'', [[Klaus]] uses the older version of the Life Giving Amulet to revive himself. * The Life Giving Amulet shares the same in-game model with the [[Construction Amulet]], but its gem is red instead of green. * Ironically, though this amulet resurrects the dead, it can still be found in graves. *This item could be a reference to the Amulet of Life Saving from ''NetHack''. == {{Pic32|Research Notes}} Assets == Life Giving Amulet Dropped.png|Dropped Life Giving Amulet Equipped.png|Equipped =={{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery== Wilserrect.gif|Wilson resurrecting while a nearby [[Birds|Redbird]] is put to sleep.
